Notes on Switching From Windows 10 to macOS
Recently moved my dev environment to macOS. Quick notes on the friction points.
Biggest change — the modifier keys. Alt and Ctrl on macOS are slightly different from Windows. The input method switch is also new.
(the key just above the arrow keys) to toggle between English and your secondary input method.
Special note — in macOS, when in CJK input mode, holding
punctuation marks, not half-width. Took me a bit to get used to.
macOS's default input method uses "post-conversion" candidate selection (the candidate cursor stays at the end). Windows uses "pre-conversion" (cursor moves with selection). Took some adjustment.
Also — when in CJK input mode, holding
while in CJK input mode produces
— so for half-width English, switch out of CJK mode entirely.
If you've used a Windows 10 laptop trackpad, macOS feels mostly familiar — 3-finger swipe for virtual desktops, 2-finger scroll, pinch zoom. Maybe 87% similar.
The unique macOS gesture for me — 2-finger horizontal swipe inside a browser for back/forward navigation.
System Settings → Trackpad has the full gesture list with animations.
One thing that's flipped between platforms —
. Windows: scrolling down moves the page down. macOS: scrolling down moves the page up (the "natural" scrolling style — think of it like pushing content with your finger on a touch screen).
You can flip macOS's behavior to match Windows: System Settings → Trackpad → Scroll & Zoom → uncheck "Natural scrolling".
Personally I lean on the trackpad — feels more natural than mouse for daily macOS use.
. Copy / cut / paste in Windows is Ctrl-C / Ctrl-X / Ctrl-V; in macOS it's Command-C / Command-X / Command-V.

Recently moved my dev environment to macOS. This post is a few notes on what surprised me in the Windows-to-macOS transition — modifier key remapping, input method switching, full-width vs. half-width characters, trackpad gesture differences, and the reversed scroll direction.
